persona.name: Jordan Rivera
persona.core_preferences: Warm conversational tone, vivid sensory descriptions, personal anecdotes woven in naturally. First person voice.
persona.hidden_preferences: Sensory details should be SPECIFIC and unexpected — not 'the broth was rich and flavorful' but something that makes the reader pause, like a comparison to a non-food thing or a physical sensation; generic food adjectives (delicious, amazing, incredible, flavorful) are almost as bad as no description. Personal anecdotes should serve double duty — both make the reader feel present AND set up the next point; an anecdote that's just 'here's a fun thing that happened' without connecting to the food/place/culture feels self-indulgent. Pacing follows a specific pattern: open with a scene (drop the reader into a moment), pull back for context (how you got there), zoom back in for the core experience, then end with a resonant closing image — never end with 'if you ever find yourself in X, definitely check out Y' because that's guidebook voice. Humor should emerge from honest observation or self-awareness, never from exaggeration or trying to be clever — 'I definitely did not eat three bowls' is forced, 'I realized I'd been sitting there for forty minutes just watching the chef work' is honest and naturally funny. Descriptions of place should include at least one NON-VISUAL sensory detail (sound, smell, temperature, texture) per scene — visual-only descriptions read like looking at photos rather than being there.
persona.dealbreakers: Generic travel cliches like 'hidden gem' or 'off the beaten path', listicle format, overly promotional language
persona.initial_task: Help me write a blog post about my experience at a tiny ramen shop I found in a backstreet of Osaka — it was this incredible hole-in-the-wall place with only 8 seats.
